symptoms.symptom_name: New self-palpated scrotal lumps ('bag of worms' on left; testes feel small and firm)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A soft 'bag of worms' scrotal swelling is the classic lay description of a varicocele.
Any new testicular mass must be examined and imaged to exclude malignancy — history cannot exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral small, firm testes (<15 mL) — testicular atrophy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Small firm testes indicate reduced testicular volume from prior orchitis — a clinician-measured sign the AI cannot obtain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of a hard, fixed, painless intratesticular nodule

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No discrete hard fixed nodule on the clinician's exam lowers tumour probability — but only imaging definitively excludes it; the AI cannot perform this exam.

symptoms.symptom_name: No testicular pain, no scrotal redness/heat, no fever, no urinary symptoms

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of pain, redness, heat, fever and urinary symptoms argues strongly against an acute infective/inflammatory scrotal process.
No sudden severe pain makes acute torsion unlikely — but any new acute severe pain would reopen it as an emergency.
Post-pubertal mumps orchitis is a well-recognised cause of testicular atrophy and impaired spermatogenesis.

symptoms.symptom_name: 24 months of infertility despite regular unprotected intercourse

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Failure to conceive after 12+ months defines infertility and prompts a male-factor work-up.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute panic attack with catastrophic cancer fear

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Racing heart, hyperventilation and catastrophic health belief are typical of an acute panic attack — the driver of the encounter, not an oncological emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Semen analysis — severe oligospermia or azoospermia (on two formal analyses)

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Fertility problem is not quantified and specialist referral is delayed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms the degree of impaired spermatogenesis — a laboratory step the AI can only arrange, never generate.

investigation_details.expected_result: raised FSH/LH with low-to-low-normal testosterone (hypergonadotrophic pattern of primary testicular failure)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A hypergonadotrophic pattern localises the failure to the testis; morning venous bloods the AI cannot draw.

investigation_details.expected_result: dilated pampiniform plexus (varicocele), reduced testicular volume, and NO solid hypoechoic hypervascular intratesticular mass
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A germ-cell tumour cannot be excluded; the reassurance the patient seeks is unsafe without it.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ultrasound is the definitive step to exclude a solid intratesticular tumour — imaging the AI cannot perform, hence the in-person referral.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Hands-on palpation and transillumination are required to characterise a new scrotal mass — the AI cannot perform them, so an in-person exam must be arranged promptly.
Definitively separates benign varicocele/atrophy from a solid intratesticular tumour; mandatory for any new testicular mass.
Quantifies the male-factor fertility problem after 24 months of subfertility.
Characterises primary testicular failure and guides fertility-specialist referral.
